data.content:
Here's the full diagnosis:

---

## Build Failure Analysis — `BuildDiag.sln`

### Solution Structure
```
BuildDiag.sln
├── Core.csproj          (net8.0, class library)
├── Services.csproj      (net8.0, class library) → references Core
└── Api.csproj           (net8.0, exe)           → references Services + Core
```

### Root Cause (1 of 1)

**`Services.csproj` — Missing NuGet package `Newtonsoft.Json`**

`Services/UserService.cs` uses `Newtonsoft.Json` (`using Newtonsoft.Json;`, `using Newtonsoft.Json.Linq;`, type `JObject`), but `Services.csproj` has **no `<PackageReference>` for `Newtonsoft.Json`**. The `project.assets.json` confirms only the `Core` project reference is resolved — no NuGet packages at all.

3 errors, all in `Services/UserService.cs`:
| Code | Line | Message |
|------|------|---------|
| CS0246 | (2,7) | `'Newtonsoft'` namespace not found |
| CS0246 | (3,7) | `'Newtonsoft'` namespace not found |
| CS0246 | (22,19) | `'JObject'` type not found |

### Cascading Failures

**`Api.csproj`** depends on `Services.csproj` via `<ProjectReference>`. While the binlog shows `Api` didn't emit its own errors in this run (MSBuild stopped the dependent build early), it **cannot succeed** until `Services` is fixed.

### Fix

Add the missing PackageReference to `Services/Services.csproj`:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <PackageReference Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This single change resolves all 3 errors. No other root causes exist — `Core.csproj` built successfully.
